items.title: Cloud Native Live: Kyverno 1.15: New Policy Types
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Join us for a follow-up session where we explore the new policy types introduced in Kyverno 1.15 — MutatingPolicies, DeletingPolicies, and GeneratingPolicies.

This builds on our previous session, where we discussed Kyverno’s shift to CEL and introduced ValidatingPolicies and ImageValidatingPolicy. We’ll dive into how these new policies work, use cases they unlock, and how they fit into the future of Kubernetes policy management.</article>

items.title: CNL: Bringing Agentic AI to Cloud Native with kagent & Kyverno
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content: <article>Let’s stop babysitting clusters and build AI agents to operate and govern for us! Kagent, a new CNCF project, enables simple declarative development of Kubernetes-native AI agents for any cloud native projects. This session will show you how to rapidly construct custom AI agents for essential operational and management tasks. Learn how Kyverno, the Kubernetes-native policy engine for managing validation, mutation, generation, and cleanup, can be extended with AI capabilities through kagent using the emerging Model Context Protocol (MCP) to enable intelligent policy recommendations and automation.</article>

items.title: CNCF Member Video Spotlight: Mirantis with Prithvi Raj
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Hear how CNCF empowered Mirantis to grow K0S and Cordent, connect with a global community, and shape the future of platform engineering.
The cloud native movement is more than tech—it’s momentum.</article>

items.title: ChatLoopBackOff: Episode 65 (Kubean)
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content: <article>Ready to simplify Kubernetes cluster management across hybrid or multi-cloud environments? In this Live demo, CNCF Ambassador Afzal Ansari explores Kubean, a CNCF project that brings an Ansible-powered, GitOps-friendly approach to Kubernetes operations. Kubean aims to streamline the full lifecycle management of Kubernetes clusters—provisioning, upgrading, scaling, and decommissioning—using declarative configurations and a plugin-based architecture. Afzal will walk through the project’s core components, check out its integration with tools like Cluster API and OpenKruise, and assess its community momentum and production readiness. If you're interested in scalable cluster automation with a flexible control plane, don’t miss this first look at what Kubean has to offer.</article>

items.title: ChatLoopBackOff: Episode 66 (Kube-OVN)
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content: <article>Kube-OVN brings the power of Open vSwitch (OVS) to Kubernetes, delivering a rich, policy-driven Container Network Interface (CNI) built for modern cloud-native infrastructure. In this livestream, a CNCF Ambassador, Koray Oksay dives into Kube-OVN for the first time—exploring how it extends native Kubernetes networking with advanced features like built-in IPAM, traffic mirroring, QoS, and a controller-driven control plane. Koray will take a look at the project’s architecture, community activity, and ecosystem integrations to understand where Kube-OVN fits in the cloud-native networking landscape. Whether you're curious about underlay/overlay support or managing complex network policies, join us as we unpack what makes Kube-OVN a compelling option for production-grade Kubernetes networks.</article>

items.title: ChatLoopBackOff: Episode 64 (Bank-Vaults)
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content: <article>Join CNCF Ambassador Shivlal Sharma as he dives into bank-vaults for the first time! A CNCF Sandbox project, bank-vaults is a powerful toolset for managing secrets securely with HashiCorp Vault in Kubernetes environments. It simplifies the deployment, configuration, and lifecycle management of Vault, offering features like automatic unsealing, dynamic configuration, and seamless integration with Kubernetes authentication. Whether you're building secure, cloud-native platforms or just starting to explore secrets management at scale, this session is a hands-on introduction to how bank-vaults brings operational ease and strong security together. Watch live as Shivlal explores how this project fits into the modern cloud-native security stack.</article>

Learn more at https://kubecon.io</article>

items.title: CNCF on-demand webinar – Solving Android app issues with OpenTelemetry: Beyond local profiling
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>OpenTelemetry enables engineers to speak a common language across app boundaries. An emerging use-case is in debugging common issues for mobile apps.

Existing tools like Android Studio Profiler provide powerful capabilities to detect and address all kinds of performance issues, such as UI thread blocking, memory leaks, or excessive CPU usage. However, these miss the user-facing issues when Android applications make it into production! Certain problems don’t show up in controlled environments, with consistent network connectivity, predictable user behavior, and a limited range of testing devices. In the real world, users interact with apps in unexpected ways, with diverse hardware, and varying conditions, exposing issues that are difficult to replicate locally.</article>

items.title: ChatLoopBackOff: Episode 63 (cloudevents)
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content: <article>Join us for Episode 63 on ChatLoopBackOff, where CNCF Ambassador Nitish Kumar dives into the world of CloudEvents for the very first time! In this interactive session, he will explore how CloudEvents is standardizing event data across services, enabling seamless interoperability in event-driven systems. Whether you're building serverless architectures, integrating across cloud platforms, or just curious about the future of event handling, this is your perfect intro. With live reactions, real-time learning, and practical experimentation, watch Nitish demystify the specification that’s powering modern cloud-native ecosystems—one event at a time.</article>

items.title: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon China 2025 • Highlights from Hong Kong
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Get a glimpse into the future of cloud native! 🚀 Highlights from KubeCon + CloudNativeCon China 2025 are here. From June 10-11 in Hong Kong, the community came together to share groundbreaking ideas, network with peers, and explore the latest advancements in Kubernetes and open source technologies.

Relive the energy, innovation, and collaboration that made this event unforgettable. View all 84 conference session videos on the CNCF YouTube channel here https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLj6h78yzYM2P1xtALqTcCmRAa6142uERl

items.title: Deaf in Cloud Native: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon EU 2025 Keynote Experience
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content: <article>Join the Deaf in Cloud Native community as we look back at our wonderful experience at KubeCon + CloudNativeCon Europe 2025 in London. This recap celebrates a landmark moment for our community - the first solo keynote delivered by one of our own, Rob Koch, CNCF DHHWG Co-Chair. In this session, Rob shares the behind-the-scenes story of his presentation, “Empowering Accessibility Through Kubernetes: The Future of Real-Time Sign Language Interpretation.” Following Rob’s story, community leaders Milad Vafaeifard and Anastasiia Gubska, a CNCF Ambassador, share their key takeaways, leading into a community roundtable where everyone shares their highlights from the conference.</article>

items.title: Spark Operator - Feature Engineering with Spark on Kubeflow - Vikas Saxena, RAICS.AI
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon India in Hyderabad (August 6-7), and KubeCon + CloudNativeCon North America in Atlanta (November 10-13). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io

Spark Operator - Feature Engineering with Spark on Kubeflow - Vikas Saxena, RAICS.AI

Real-world ML rarely deals with clean tables—more often, it involves messy inputs like PDFs, scanned documents, images, ZIP files, and data from enterprise warehouses. In this session, we’ll explore how to transform that diverse data into model-ready features using Apache Spark with the Kubeflow Spark Operator, all orchestrated through Kubeflow Pipelines. We’ll walk through how this approach bridges a previous gap in Kubeflow: extracting actionable insights from massive volumes of raw data—hundreds of terabytes—using fully open-source tools and technologies. Target Audience: Data and ML engineers with basic Spark or Kubernetes experience.</article>

items.title: Simplifying Generative AI Model Training on Kubernetes using Helm Charts - Ajay Vohra & Omri Shiv
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon India in Hyderabad (August 6-7), and KubeCon + CloudNativeCon North America in Atlanta (November 10-13). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io

Simplifying Generative AI Model Training on Kubernetes using Helm Charts - Ajay Vohra & Omri Shiv, AWS

Training generative AI models on Kubernetes offers a wide range of frameworks, tools, and orchestration options. While this diversity fuels innovation, it also introduces significant complexity. In this talk, we present a Helm-based approach that simplifies AI model training using Kubeflow Training Operators. This method abstracts much of the underlying complexity while preserving flexibility in choosing training technologies. Our solution is accelerator-agnostic and provides a consistent YAML interface across various training frameworks. We’ll also introduce a new Kubeflow Pipeline component that enables the construction of complex, end-to-end training workflows using Helm charts. Through real-world examples, we’ll showcase training pipelines using Accelerate, Ray Train + Lightning, and NVIDIA’s NeMo-Megatron libraries. We’ll also demonstrate automatic scaling of accelerator infrastructure using Karpenter.</article>
